codeReview.summary: GitHub Actions workflow created at .github/workflows/ui-md-validation.yml that meets all validation assertions. Workflow triggers on push/PR to main for .md and tooling changes, runs linter on sample files, runs test suite, performs link checking with lychee, and uses minimal permissions (read-all).

sharedStateObservations.observation: AGENTS.md section 8.5 provides a CI integration example that differs slightly from the implementation. The example uses 'on: push: paths:' without specifying branches, while the implementation correctly adds 'branches: [main]' for push and includes pull_request trigger. This is a good deviation - the worker correctly recognized that push without branch restriction would cause duplicate runs for PRs targeting main.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: AGENTS.md:481-495 shows example without branch restriction; implementation adds 'branches: [main]' which is more correct

sharedStateObservations.observation: The workflow self-triggers when the workflow file itself changes (includes '.github/workflows/ui-md-validation.yml' in paths). This is appropriate and ensures the workflow is tested when modified.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: .github/workflows/ui-md-validation.yml:9-11 includes the workflow file in paths

sharedStateObservations.observation: The tooling-worker skill procedure for CI features wasn't explicitly defined beyond 'CI workflow setup' in the description, but worker correctly followed the general pattern of checking existing samples and implementing according to feature requirements.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Skill description says 'CI workflow setup' as a use case; worker followed general implement-test-commit pattern

summary: Feature implementation passes review. Workflow file created at .github/workflows/ui-md-validation.yml with three parallel jobs (lint, test, link-check). All VAL-CI assertions satisfied: VAL-CI-001 (workflow exists), VAL-CI-002 (runs linter on sample files), VAL-CI-003 (minimal permissions read-all). No blocking issues identified.
